blocks.body: Past Perfect: Time Expressions — before, after, by the time, already, just, never, before that, until then.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"Words and expressions like before, after, by the time, already, just, never, before that, until then often point to Past Perfect.","rules":[{"label":"Before / After","color":"emerald","text":"These words help show the order of two events in the past.","example":"She had left before I arrived. / After he had finished, he went out."},{"label":"By the time","color":"blue","text":"This expression shows that the action was already complete before another past moment.","example":"By the time we got there, the shop had closed."},{"label":"Already / Just / Never","color":"amber","text":"These words add detail about the state of the action before another past moment.","example":"They had already eaten. / She had just left. / I had never seen it before."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common time markers for Past Perfect.","items":[{"label":"Order","title":"before / after","subtitle":"before I arrived, after he had finished"},{"label":"Past point","title":"by the time / before that / until then","subtitle":"By the time we called, they had left."},{"label":"Already / Just","title":"already / just","subtitle":"She had already eaten. / He had just arrived."},{"label":"Experience","title":"never","subtitle":"I had never seen snow before that trip."}]}

{"title":"2. How these words work","sections":[{"label":"before / after","color":"emerald","description":"They help you clearly show the order of two events in the past.","examples":[{"en":"She had packed her bag before the taxi came.","ua":"Packing happened first."},{"en":"After they had finished dinner, they watched a film.","ua":"Dinner came first, the film came later."}]},{"label":"by the time / before that / until then","color":"blue","description":"These expressions show that a result already existed by a certain moment in the past.","examples":[{"en":"By the time I arrived, the lesson had started.","ua":"The lesson was already in progress by then."},{"en":"Until then, we had never heard that story.","ua":"There was no earlier experience before that point."}]},{"label":"already / just / never","color":"amber","description":"These words add detail about how complete or recent the earlier action was.","examples":[{"en":"They had already left before the storm began.","ua":"The earlier action was already complete."},{"en":"She had just sat down when the phone rang.","ua":"The action happened a very short time before."}]},{"label":"before that","color":"violet","description":"This expression often takes the story back to an even earlier moment.","examples":[{"en":"I had met him once before that.","ua":"There was an earlier meeting in the past."},{"en":"He had never spoken about it before that evening.","ua":"There was no earlier mention before that point."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common combinations","intro":"See how these time expressions work with Past Perfect.","rows":[{"en":"She had left before I arrived.","ua":"order of two events","note":"before"},{"en":"After they had finished work, they went home.","ua":"one action first, the next one later","note":"after"},{"en":"By the time we got there, the train had gone.","ua":"the result was already complete","note":"by the time"},{"en":"I had never seen that place before that trip.","ua":"experience before a later moment","note":"never / before that"}],"warning":"With when or by the time, Past Perfect often goes with Past Simple for the second event: they had left when I arrived."}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["before and after help show the order of events in the past.","by the time, before that, and until then show a past limit before which something had or had not happened.","already, just, and never add detail about the earlier action.","With these time markers, Past Perfect often sounds the most natural."]}
